messages.content: Hello, what brings you to the emergency room today?

Doc, I'm so glad I'm here. I've been having this terrible chest pain. It's like an elephant sitting on my chest, if you know what I mean. It's been going on for about an hour now. I was just sitting in my living room, watching TV, and suddenly I felt this...this pressure. It's hard to describe. It's not just my chest, it's my whole upper body. I'm also sweating a lot, and it's hard to catch my breath. I'm trying to calm down, but it's really scaring me. I'm 67, I've never felt anything like this before. Can you help me, please?
Can you describe the chest pain for me? What does it feel like?

It's like...have you ever been in a tight spot, where you feel like you can't move? That's what it feels like. It's a heavy, crushing pressure. It's not sharp or stabbing, but more like a dull ache. It's like someone's sitting on my chest, pressing down on me. It's hard to breathe, and every time I inhale, it feels like I'm going to get squished. It's really uncomfortable. And it's not just my chest, my arms and shoulders feel it too, like they're being squeezed. It's a bit hard to explain, but it's just this awful, oppressive feeling. (takes a deep breath) Sorry, I'm getting a bit winded just talking about it.
Where exactly do you feel the pain?

The pain is mostly in the center of my chest, just behind my breastbone. It's like it's radiating out from there, into my shoulders and arms. It's not just a small area, it's more like it's wrapping around my whole upper body. Sometimes it feels like it's moving, like it's spreading to my back or my jaw, but it's still centered in the chest. (pauses, takes a deep breath) Sorry, it's hard to pinpoint exactly. It's just a big, heavy feeling that's hard to ignore.
When did the pain start, and how long has it been going on?

The pain started about an hour ago, while I was sitting in my living room, watching TV. I was just sitting there, feeling fine, and then suddenly I felt this...this pressure. It's been constant since then. It hasn't really gone away, it's just been building up. I've tried to calm down, take some deep breaths, but it's still here. (looks around the room, a bit anxious) I'm getting a bit worried, to be honest.
On a scale of 1 to 10, how bad is the pain right now?

It's a 9 out of 10. It's really bad. It's like it's bearing down on me, making it hard to think straight. I'm trying to stay calm, but it's not easy. Even talking about it is making me feel a bit worse, to be honest. (pauses, takes a deep breath) Sorry, I'm just trying to get through this. It's just really uncomfortable.
Does anything make the pain better or worse, like resting or moving?

Well, it seems like the pain is a bit worse when I'm moving around or taking deep breaths. When I'm still, it's not as bad. But even just sitting still, the pain is still there, it's just...it's more manageable, I suppose. (pauses) When I move, it's like it gets a bit worse, like it's getting a bit more...agitated, I guess. And the sweating, it's worse when I'm moving too. It's like my body is trying to tell me something, but I'm not sure what. (looks at you with concern) Does that make sense?
Are you having any other symptoms, like shortness of breath or sweating?

Yes, I am having a lot of trouble breathing. It's hard to catch my breath, like my lungs are working overtime. And I'm sweating a lot, like I said. My armpits, my forehead, everywhere. It's like my body is trying to cool itself down or something. (looks around, a bit worried) I'm also feeling a bit tingling in my arms, like pins and needles, but it's not as bad as the chest pain. (pauses, takes a deep breath) I'm just trying to stay calm and focus on getting through this.
Do you have any medical conditions like high blood pressure or high cholesterol?

I've got high blood pressure, but I've been managing it with medication. And I've also got high cholesterol, but I've been on a special diet and exercising regularly to try and keep it under control. I've also quit smoking about 10 years ago, so that's a plus. (pauses, looks around the room) I'm not sure if that's relevant, but I thought I'd mention it.
What medications are you currently taking?

Let me think for a moment... I'm taking lisinopril for my high blood pressure, and simvastatin for my cholesterol. I also take a multivitamin and a baby aspirin every day. That's about it. I'm not taking any other medications right now. (looks at you with a concerned expression) Is that relevant to what's going on with me?
Does anyone in your family have a history of heart disease?

My father had a heart attack when he was in his mid-60s. He passed away from complications related to heart disease. I've always been a bit concerned about my own heart health, but I've been trying to take care of myself. (pauses, looks down) It's just...I don't want to go down that road, you know? I want to make sure I do everything I can to stay healthy.
Hello, what brings you to the emergency room today?

Hi, thank you for seeing me. I'm having some... well, it's not exactly what I'd call fun, but I've been feeling a lot of pressure in my chest. It's been about an hour, I think. I was climbing the stairs at my office building, and suddenly I just felt this... heaviness in my chest. It's like someone's sitting on me, if you know what I mean. It's not going away, and I'm starting to feel a bit anxious about it. I'm worried it might be my heart or something. I've had high blood pressure for years, but I've been managing it with medication. I'm a bit concerned, to be honest.
Can you describe the chest pain for me? What does it feel like?

It's a bit hard to describe, but it's like a squeezing or a pressure, like someone's constricting my chest. It's not a sharp pain, more of a dull, heavy feeling. It's like my chest is being compressed, you know? It's not constant, but it's there, even when I'm not moving or doing anything. It's like it's just... there. And it's not just my chest, it's a bit in my arms too, but more in my chest. It's a bit hard to breathe when I think about it, to be honest.
Where exactly do you feel the pain?

The pain is mostly in the center of my chest, right behind my sternum. It's a bit harder to pinpoint, but if I had to draw a line, I'd say it's about an inch or two below the center of my chest, if that makes sense. It's not radiating to my shoulders or back, just kind of... contained in my chest.
When did the pain start, and how long has it been going on?

Like I said, it started about an hour ago, when I was climbing the stairs. I was on the 10th floor, and I was taking the stairs because I like to get some exercise. As soon as I got to the top, I felt this... sensation in my chest, and it's been there ever since. It's not getting worse, but it's not getting better either. It's just kind of... steady.
On a scale of 1 to 10, how bad is the pain right now?

I'd say it's about a 6 out of 10. It's not excruciating or anything, but it's definitely uncomfortable. It's a bit distracting, to be honest. And it's making me a bit anxious, like I said. If I'm being completely honest, it's a bit more than just a 6, but I don't want to overreact. I'm trying to stay calm and see what happens.
Does anything make the pain better or worse, like resting or moving?

Well, when I'm sitting still, the pain is a bit more manageable. It's still there, but it's not as intense. When I move around or try to take deep breaths, it gets a bit worse. And like I said, climbing those stairs was when it started, so maybe that's a trigger or something. But sitting still, just relaxing, seems to make it a bit more bearable.
Are you having any other symptoms, like shortness of breath or sweating?

Yeah, I am having a bit of trouble breathing. It's not like I'm gasping for air or anything, but it's a bit harder to get a good breath in. It's like my chest is just a bit more... resistant to letting me breathe, I guess. And yeah, I've been sweating a bit too. It's not pouring or anything, but I can feel the sweat on my forehead and upper lip. It's a bit uncomfortable, to be honest.
Do you have any medical conditions like high blood pressure or high cholesterol?

I've got high blood pressure, yeah. I've been managing it with medication, Lisinopril 10mg. I've been taking it as prescribed, and my blood pressure's been under control for the most part. I'm also supposed to watch my diet and exercise regularly, but I haven't been doing as well as I should on that front, to be honest.
What medications are you currently taking?
I'm taking Lisinopril 10mg once a day, as I mentioned. That's it, just that one medication for my high blood pressure. I'm pretty consistent with taking it, so I don't think that's the issue here.
Does anyone in your family have a history of heart disease?

My dad had a heart attack when I was in my early twenties, so I've always been a bit worried about my own heart health. He had bypass surgery and was okay, but it made me more aware of my own risks, you know? My mom's side of the family's got a history of high blood pressure and heart issues too, so I've always tried to take care of myself.
Hello, what brings you to the emergency room today?

Hello there, doc. I'm not sure what's going on, but I've been feeling some pressure in my chest for about an hour now. It's been getting worse, and I'm getting a little anxious about it. I've also been sweating a lot and having a hard time catching my breath. I've tried to calm myself down, but it's not getting any better. I'm not sure what's causing it, but I'm hoping you can help me figure it out.
Can you describe the chest pain for me? What does it feel like?

It's a squeezing feeling, like someone's got a tight band around my chest. It's not sharp or stabbing, but more of a steady pressure. It's hard to describe, but it's like my chest is being compressed. It's not just my chest, either - it feels like it's radiating down my left arm, too. That's a bit uncomfortable, but the pressure in my chest is the main concern.
Where exactly do you feel the pain?

The pain is mainly in the center of my chest, just behind my sternum. It's a bit harder to pinpoint, but if I had to say, it's about an inch or two to the left of the middle of my chest. And like I mentioned earlier, it does radiate down my left arm, into my shoulder and down towards my elbow.
When did the pain start, and how long has it been going on?

It started about an hour ago, I'd say. I was just sitting at home, watching TV, and suddenly I felt this pressure building up in my chest. It's been steady since then, with maybe a slight increase in intensity over time. I've been trying to calm down and relax, but it's not getting any better.
On a scale of 1 to 10, how bad is the pain right now?

I'd say it's a 6 out of 10. It's uncomfortable, but I'm trying to stay calm and focused. The sweating and difficulty breathing are more concerning to me than the pain itself, to be honest. But the pain is definitely noticeable and distracting.
Does anything make the pain better or worse, like resting or moving?

To be honest, it seems like the pain is pretty consistent, but it might be a tiny bit worse when I take a deep breath in. When I'm sitting still, it's more manageable, but as soon as I try to take a deep breath or move around, it seems to get a bit more pronounced. Resting doesn't seem to make it go away, but it doesn't make it worse either. It's just... steady, I guess.
Are you having any other symptoms, like shortness of breath or sweating?

Yeah, I am having some shortness of breath. It's not as bad as it was when I first started feeling the pressure in my chest, but it's still there. I'd say it's a 4 out of 10 in terms of severity. And, like I mentioned earlier, I'm sweating a lot. It's not just a little bit, either - I'm drenched. I've been trying to cool off, but nothing seems to be helping.
Do you have any medical conditions like high blood pressure or high cholesterol?

Yeah, I've got a history of hypertension. I've been managing it with medication, Lisinopril 10mg once a day. I've been pretty consistent with taking it, but I'm not sure if that's related to what's going on right now.
What medications are you currently taking?

In addition to the Lisinopril I mentioned, I'm also taking a multivitamin and a low-dose aspirin daily. I've also got a prescription for a beta blocker, but I don't take it as regularly as I probably should. And, of course, I've got my Lisinopril.
Does anyone in your family have a history of heart disease?
My father had a heart attack when I was a teenager, and he's been living with coronary artery disease ever since. He's been doing okay, but it's definitely made me more aware of my own risk factors.
What brings you in today?

Doc, I'm really worried about this. I was driving my truck, just sitting there, and suddenly I felt this... this tightness in my chest. It's like someone's squeezing my heart or something. It's been about 30 minutes now, and it's been getting worse. I tried to shake it off, but it's not going away. And I'm getting these cold sweats, too. My palms are all sweaty, and my heart's racing. I'm having trouble catchin' my breath, like I'm not gettin' enough air. It's really scaring me, doc. I've had high blood pressure for a while, but I've been takin' my meds like I'm supposed to. I'm worried it might be related to that.
Tell me more about what you're feeling.

It's like... have you ever felt like you're being squeezed really tight? That's what it feels like. It's in my chest, just behind my breastbone. It's not sharp, it's more like a dull ache, but it's getting worse. And it's making it hard to breathe, like my chest is getting smaller or something. I've been tryin' to take deep breaths, but it's like my lungs are gettin' stuck. And these cold sweats... I'm shakin' a little bit, too. I'm tryin' to stay calm, but it's hard. I'm gettin' a little lightheaded, too. I'm worried I might pass out or somethin'.

You know, I've been feelin' okay lately, just the usual truck driver stuff. Long hours, stress, that kinda thing. But this is somethin' else. I don't know what's goin' on
Can you point to where it hurts?

It's... it's right here *points to the center of his chest*. You know, right behind my breastbone. It's like a big, tight band around my heart. If I press on it, it hurts a little more, but if I relax, it's a little better. But it's always there, doc. It's not goin' away. *takes a deep breath and winces slightly*
When did this all start?

Like I said, it started about 30 minutes ago. I was drivin' my truck, just cruisin' down the highway, and suddenly I felt this... this thing. I don't know, it just came out of nowhere. I pulled over to the side of the road, and I've been sittin' here in the truck ever since, tryin' to catch my breath. It's been gettin' worse, like I said. I'm gettin' a little more anxious by the minute, to be honest with you.
How would you rate the pain?

It's pretty bad, doc. I'd say it's a 7 or 8 out of 10. It's not like a sharp pain, like when I stub my toe or somethin'. It's more like a dull ache, like someone's squeezin' my heart. It's hard to describe, but it's just... it's uncomfortable, you know? It's like my chest is constricted or somethin'. When I breathe in, it feels like it's gettin' worse, and when I breathe out, it feels like it's gettin' a little better, but not much.
Does anything change the pain at all?

Well, like I said, when I breathe in, it gets a little worse. But when I breathe out, it's a tiny bit better. And if I lean forward a little bit, it's a tiny bit better too. But if I sit back and relax, it gets a little worse again. It's like it's connected to my breathing, somehow. And if I press on my chest, like I was tellin' you before, it hurts a little more. But if I stop pressin' on it, it's a little better. It's all pretty weird, doc.
Anything else going on that's bothering you?

Well, besides the chest pain, I've been gettin' these cold sweats like I said. And I'm gettin' a little lightheaded, like I might pass out or somethin'. And it's hard to catch my breath, like I mentioned before. That's about it, doc. Just the chest pain and those other things. I'm a little worried, to be honest. I've never felt anything like this before.
Any medical problems in the past?

Yeah, I've got high blood pressure, like I mentioned before. I've been takin' Lisinopril for it. I've also had some problems with my back, just from years of drivin' a truck. And I've had a few allergies, but that's about it. I'm allergic to penicillin, so I gotta be careful with antibiotics. But that's it, doc. Nothing too serious, just the usual stuff.
Are you taking any medications or supplements?

Yeah, I'm on Lisinopril for my blood pressure. I take 10mg once a day. That's it, doc. Just that one pill. I'm pretty regular with it, too. I don't take any other medications or supplements, just the Lisinopril.
Any health problems that run in the family?

My dad had a heart attack when he was in his 50s. He had bypass surgery and was okay after that, but it was a wake-up call for him, you know? He's still goin' strong, but I've been tryin' to take care of myself, just in case. My mom had some problems with her heart too, but I don't know all the details. She's passed away, so I don't really know much about her medical history.
What brings you in today?

Doc, I'm really worried about my chest. I've been feelin' this terrible squeezing pain in my chest for about an hour now. It's like someone's got a tight grip on my heart. It's not goin' away, and it's gettin' worse. I'm gettin' a little cold sweats, too. And I've got this weird tinglin' sensation down my left arm. It's scaring me, doc. I've never felt anything like this before. I was just sittin' in my truck, mindin' my own business, and then this hits me. I'm gettin' a little anxious, if you know what I mean. Can you help me figure out what's goin' on?
Tell me more about what you're feeling.

It's hard to explain, doc. The pain is like a tight band around my chest. It's not sharp, it's more like a pressure that won't quit. Sometimes it gets a little worse, and I can feel my heart racin' in my chest. I've been tryin' to breathe deep, but it's hard to catch my breath. And that tinglin' in my arm, it's like a numbness, but not quite. It's hard to describe.

When I sit up or move around, the pain gets a little better, but it's still there. It's like my body's tryin' to tell me somethin', but I don't know what. I've been worried sick, doc. I've never felt anything like this before. I'm gettin' a little lightheaded, too. I don't know what's goin' on, and it's scaring me. Can you please help me figure out
Can you point to where it hurts?

Doc, it's hard to pinpoint, but it's like a big ol' squeeze around my chest, right in the middle. It's not just in one spot, it's more like it's encompassin' my whole chest. If I had to point, I'd say it's right behind my sternum, but it's not just there, it's all around. And that tinglin' sensation in my arm, it's like it's startin' from my shoulder, down my bicep, and into my forearm. It's a weird feelin', doc. Can I sit up a little more? This position's makin' me a bit uncomfortable.
When did this all start?

It started about an hour ago, doc. I was just drivin' my truck, mindin' my own business, and suddenly I felt this...this thing. I don't know, it just hit me out of nowhere. I pulled over to the side of the road, and I've been sittin' here ever since, tryin' to figure out what's goin' on. At first, it was just the chest pain, and I thought maybe I was just gettin' a little anxious or somethin', but then the sweatin' started, and the arm tinglin'...it's been gettin' worse from there. I'm gettin' a little worried, doc. I don't know what's goin' on, but I don't like it one bit.
How would you rate the pain?

Doc, I'd say it's a 6 out of 10. It's not gettin' any better, and it's gettin' a little worse. It's like a steady pressure that's just bearin' down on me. Sometimes it feels like it's goin' to get worse, and that's what's really got me scared. I've been tryin' to breathe deep, and that helps a bit, but it's still there. I'd rather it was a 2 or a 3, but it's a 6, and I'm gettin' a little anxious about it.
Does anything change the pain at all?

Well, doc, when I sit up or move around, the pain gets a little better, but it's not like it goes away. It's more like it's still there, but it's not as bad. And when I take deep breaths, like you told me to, it helps a bit too. It's like it's a little more manageable when I'm breathin' deep. But if I try to stand up or move around too much, it gets a little worse again. And if I put my hands on my chest or try to lean forward, it gets a little more uncomfortable. It's like my body's tryin' to tell me somethin', but I don't know what.
Anything else going on that's bothering you?

Just the anxiety, doc. I'm gettin' a little worried about what's goin' on, and it's makin' me feel a bit lightheaded. I'm tryin' to stay calm, but it's hard when you're feelin' like this. Other than that, I'm just a bit short of breath, like I've been talkin' a lot. That's about it, doc. Just the chest pain, the sweatin', and the arm tinglin'. That's all I'm aware of, anyway.
Any medical problems in the past?

Well, doc, I've been healthy as a horse, mostly. Never had any major problems, just the usual stuff. I've had my share of colds and flu, but nothin' serious. I've had a few minor injuries from work, but nothin' that's ever landed me in the hospital. I've been a truck driver for a long time, so I've had my share of bumps and bruises, but nothin' too serious. I did have a bit of a problem with allergies when I was younger, but I outgrew that. And I'm allergic to sulfa, so I gotta watch out for that. Other than that, I'm just a regular fella, doc.
Are you taking any medications or supplements?

No, doc, I'm not takin' any medications or supplements. I've never been one to take a lot of pills. I figure if I'm healthy, I don't need 'em. I do drink a lot of coffee, but that's about it. I'm a pretty straightforward fella, don't like a lot of fuss. I just take care of myself and get on with my day.
Any health problems that run in the family?

Doc, my dad had some heart problems when he was younger. He had a bypass surgery, and it seemed to take care of him for a while. But I don't know if that's related to what's goin' on with me right now. My mom had some high blood pressure, but she's doin' okay. I don't know if that's relevant, but I thought I'd mention it. Other than that, I don't know of any other health problems in the family. Just my dad's heart stuff, and that's a long time ago.
What brings you in today?

I'm feeling really anxious about this. I was climbing the stairs at my office building and suddenly I felt this... this tightness in my chest. It's like my chest is being squeezed or something. It's hard to describe. I've never felt anything like it before. I tried to shake it off, thinking it was just my heart rate going up from the exercise, but it's not going away. I'm really worried now. I'm having trouble catching my breath too. (takes a deep breath) Sorry, I'm getting a bit lightheaded.
Tell me more about what you're feeling.

It's just... my chest feels really tight, like someone is squeezing it. And my heart is racing, I can feel it. It's hard to breathe, like I'm not getting enough air. I'm getting a bit of a sweat going on, too. I'm not sure what's causing it, but it's really scaring me. I'm trying to calm down, but it's hard when you're feeling this way. (takes a few quick, shallow breaths) Oh, and my arm, on my left side, it's tingling a bit. It's not painful, but it's... unsettling. (looks worried)
Can you point to where it hurts?

(puts hand on chest) It's right here, in the center of my chest. (taps on chest) It's like a tight band or a squeeze. I don't know how to describe it any better. It's not a sharp pain, but more of a... a pressure. (points to arm) And this tingling sensation is in my left arm, just below the shoulder. (taps on arm) It's not too bad, but it's definitely there.
When did this all start?

(takes a deep breath) It started about 30 minutes ago, when I was climbing the stairs at work. I was on my way to the 5th floor, and I felt this... this tightness in my chest. I thought maybe it was just my heart rate going up from the exercise, so I stopped for a minute, sat down on the stairs, and tried to catch my breath. But it didn't go away, so I came here.
How would you rate the pain?

(hesitates, then nods) It's... 8 out of 10. I know that sounds high, but it's hard to explain. It's not a sharp pain, but it's a constant pressure that's just... bothering me. It's hard to breathe, and the tingling in my arm is making me a bit anxious. If I'm being honest, it's really affecting my day. (looks worried) I just want to feel better.
Does anything change the pain at all?

(thinks for a moment) Actually, when I sit down or lean forward, the pain seems to ease up a bit. Like, if I lean forward in this chair, it's a little more manageable. But as soon as I sit back up or stand up, it gets worse again. And if I take a few deep breaths, it helps a bit too. But it's not like the pain goes away completely, it just... eases up a bit. (looks for reassurance) Does that make sense?
Anything else going on that's bothering you?

(thinks for a moment) Well, I've been feeling a bit anxious lately, to be honest. I've been under a lot of stress with work, trying to meet deadlines and whatnot. But I've been trying to manage it with meditation and whatnot. I don't know if it's related, but it's definitely been on my mind lately. (looks down, fidgeting slightly) Oh, and I've been taking this medication, Atorvastatin, for my cholesterol. But I don't think that's related to this, I just thought I'd mention it.
Any medical problems in the past?

(nods) Yeah, I have high cholesterol, and I've been taking Atorvastatin for a few years now. That's about it, really. I've been pretty healthy, knock on wood. I used to smoke, but I quit about 10 years ago, and I've been doing pretty well since then. (looks concerned) I've been trying to take care of myself, but I guess I didn't realize how important it was to manage my stress levels... (trails off, looking worried)
Are you taking any medications or supplements?

(nods) Yes, I'm taking Atorvastatin 40mg, like I mentioned earlier. That's for my high cholesterol. I'm not taking any other medications or supplements, unless you count the occasional pain reliever for headaches. (pauses) I don't remember taking anything else recently, though. I'm pretty sure I'm not taking anything else that could be related to this... (looks worried)
Any health problems that run in the family?

(thinks for a moment) Well, my father had heart disease, and he had a heart attack when I was in my early twenties. He's doing okay now, but it was a wake-up call for me to take care of my health. My mother had high blood pressure, and she's been managing it with medication for years. Other than that, I don't know of any major health issues in my family. (looks concerned) I guess I've been thinking about my dad's experience a lot lately, and it's making me a bit anxious.
So what's going on today?

You know, I'm not really sure. I was just sitting on the couch, watching TV, and then suddenly I felt this... this... (tapping my chest) pressure. Like, it's hard to describe. It's like someone's sitting on my chest, but it's not just my lungs, if you know what I mean. It's more like... (pausing, looking for the right words)...my whole chest is constricted. And then I started sweating, and my heart's racing, and I'm having a hard time catching my breath. (taking a deep breath) I'm getting a little... (pausing, looking around the room)...queasy, too. You know, like my stomach's doing flips or something.

Now, I know I'm 57, and I've been feeling a bit... (shrugging) you know, not as spry as I used to be. But this... this is different. This is like...
Can you tell me more about that?

(sighing) Yeah, sure. Well, like I said, I was just sitting there, watching TV, and then this pressure started building up. At first, I thought maybe I just needed to, you know, move around or something. But no, it didn't go away. And then the sweating started, and my heart was racing, and I'm thinking to myself, "What's going on here?" (rubbing my chest) It's like my chest is all tight and stuff.

And the breathing thing, that's the worst part. I feel like I'm trying to catch my breath, but it's like my lungs are all... (gesturing with my hands) you know, not working right. It's hard to explain. (pausing, looking around the room) I'm getting a little... (pausing again) anxious, I guess. This isn't like me. I'm a pretty calm guy, but this is... (shaking head)
Anything else?

(thinking for a moment) Yeah, I've been feeling a bit... (pausing, trying to find the right words) anxious, like I said. And I'm getting a little... (rubbing my head) dizzy, I guess. It's like my head is spinning or something. (laughing nervously) I know it sounds silly, but it's like I'm getting lightheaded or something.

And (pausing, looking down) I've been having some... (hesitating) thoughts, I guess. You know, like, what if this is something serious? What if I'm having a heart attack or something? (looking up) I know it's probably just my imagination, but... (shrugging) I don't know, it's just hard to shake this feeling.

(looking around the room again) I've been taking my medication, of course. Lisinopril and Atorvastatin. (pausing) I've
How have you been feeling otherwise?

(shrugging) Well, I've been feeling pretty good, considering. You know, my back's been acting up a bit, but that's just from all the gardening I've been doing. (chuckling) I've been trying to get back into it, but it's not like I used to be. (laughing) I'm not as spry as I was when I was 40, that's for sure.
